metadata.question: How well does IGNIS think and reason?

nodes.label: Recommendation 2
nodes.summary: Deepen cross-pillar synergy — strengthen weak pillars, build ecosystem connections

nodes.label: Recommendation 3
nodes.summary: FORESIGHT needs attention (D) — Improve prediction accuracy and extend time horizons

nodes.label: Recommendation 4
nodes.summary: SYNTHESIS needs attention (D) — Elevate canon entries and increase pattern coverage

nodes.label: Recommendation 5
nodes.summary: EXECUTION needs attention (D) — Improve commit discipline and add test investment

edges.rationale: Source Breadth contributes to PERCEPTION
Number of non-standard custom adapters connected
Number of unique data source adapters connected
Event Volume contributes to PERCEPTION
Number of git commit events ingested
Number of CI/CD pipeline events ingested
Signal Richness contributes to PERCEPTION
Average range (max - min) of values per signal type
Average R-squared between co-occurring signal pairs
Metadata Completeness contributes to PERCEPTION
Percentage of session events with IGNIS flags
Percentage of events with full source provenance (adapter + sourceId + sourceUrl)
Temporal Coverage contributes to PERCEPTION
Balance of weekend vs weekday events (ideal ~30%)
Percentage of calendar days (within window) that have at least one event
Data Quality contributes to PERCEPTION
Percentage of events with anomalous quality scores (lower is better)
Percentage of events sharing a content hash (lower is better)
Data Provenance contributes to PERCEPTION
Number of entities observed by multiple adapters
Freshness of the most recent event (exponential decay)
Noise Filtering contributes to PERCEPTION
Rate of low-confidence events (lower is better)
Percentage of events with zero signals and low quality (lower is better)
Coverage Gaps contributes to PERCEPTION
Uniformity of coverage across adapters (balanced event counts)
Number of standard source types NOT connected (0 missing = 100)
Perception Depth contributes to PERCEPTION
Number of signals with context objects (enriched signals)
Number of signals derived from computation rather than direct observation
Session Depth contributes to TRAJECTORY
Sessions per week, ideal ~2
Ratio of sessions marked as deep work
Score Stability contributes to TRAJECTORY
Score range in recent window — smaller is more stable
Confidence in stability measurement based on data points
Velocity Trajectory contributes to TRAJECTORY
How predictable velocity is from session to session
How strong the velocity trend is (R² × direction)
Intent Coherence contributes to TRAJECTORY
Consecutive sessions with intent achievement
Percentage of sessions with tracked intents
Predictability Index contributes to TRAJECTORY
Number of predictions that have been verified
Percentage of sessions with predictions made
Session Type Distribution contributes to TRAJECTORY
Distance from ideal session type distribution
Is session type diversity increasing over time
Trend Decomposition contributes to TRAJECTORY
Magnitude of seasonal patterns — some is healthy
Primary frequency component of the score series
Inflection Detection contributes to TRAJECTORY
Mean magnitude of inflection points
Number of detected inflection points
Goal Alignment contributes to TRAJECTORY
Number of explicit goals from task board items
Percentage of goals achieved (mirrors intent achievement)
Trajectory Confidence contributes to TRAJECTORY
Number of identified growth opportunities
Number of identified risk factors — fewer is better
Intent Resolution contributes to EXECUTION
Slope of achievement rate over recent sessions
How often redirected intents lead to eventual achievement
Delivery Volume contributes to EXECUTION
Total number of shipped improvements across all sessions
Highest improvement count in a single session
Delivery Diversity contributes to EXECUTION
Number of distinct improvement group types observed
Shannon entropy of improvement group distribution
Commit Discipline contributes to EXECUTION
Total number of commits across all sessions
Inverse CV of commit intervals (regular cadence = higher score)
Test Investment contributes to EXECUTION
Most recent total number of tests in the project
Variety of test types (unit, integration, e2e, etc.)
Debt Management contributes to EXECUTION
Percentage of debt items that are properly documented
Evidence of active debt tracking and monitoring
Brainstorm Conversion contributes to EXECUTION
Total brainstorm ideas that became shipped improvements
Percentage of brainstorm ideas that ship
Efficiency contributes to EXECUTION
Inverse CV of throughput -- consistent throughput is better
Average duration of uninterrupted productive work
Quality Assurance contributes to EXECUTION
Depth and completeness of code reviews
Return on quality investments (prevention vs cure)
Execution Rhythm contributes to EXECUTION
Inverse CV of lead times -- consistent lead times are better
Percentage of planned work completed per sprint/session
Event Recency contributes to VITALITY
Exponential decay from last commit event
Exponential decay from last event of any type
Session Recency contributes to VITALITY
Trend in sessions per week over time
Consistency of session intervals - low CV = high score
Cadence Alignment contributes to VITALITY
Consecutive sessions within ideal cadence range
R-squared of intervals - how predictable is the cadence
Momentum Vector contributes to VITALITY
Slope of IQ scores over sessions
Total events in the last week (log scale)
File Staleness contributes to VITALITY
Freshness of config files - proxy from config-related events
Freshness of build/deploy artifacts - proxy from commit and build events
Recovery Responsiveness contributes to VITALITY
Structural recovery pattern - do recoveries follow a consistent shape
Average days to recover after a gap
Metabolic Rate contributes to VITALITY
Quality of work-rest cycles from event spacing patterns
Output per unit of energy - velocity per event count
Health Indicators contributes to VITALITY
Infrastructure and build health from commit and build event patterns
Community engagement from review and collaboration events
Burnout Detection contributes to VITALITY
Adequate rest between work sessions from gap patterns
Rate of velocity decline - higher score = less decline = less burnout risk
Seasonal Patterns contributes to VITALITY
Activity concentration by time of day
Activity concentration by day of week
Portfolio Context contributes to SYNTHESIS
Evenness of health scores across pillars
Rate of portfolio expansion from event volume trend
Pattern Propagation contributes to SYNTHESIS
Number of patterns shared across projects
Patterns that apply to multiple projects
Canon Authority contributes to SYNTHESIS
Number of canon entries applicable to current project
How frequently canon entries are applied
Decision Network contributes to SYNTHESIS
Rate of decision pattern reuse from recurring tags
Rate of validated decisions from test and review events
Genome Readiness contributes to SYNTHESIS
Foundational patterns identified for genome from canon entries
Readiness for knowledge transfer from documented patterns and canon
Prediction Ecosystem contributes to SYNTHESIS
Total number of forge proposals generated
Percentage of forge proposals approved
Knowledge Graph contributes to SYNTHESIS
Tags that appear in many events (hub nodes)
Density of the knowledge graph from edge/node ratio
Insight Cross-Pollination contributes to SYNTHESIS
Rate of insights applied across contexts from canon applicability
Success rate of adapted insights from implementation evidence
Ecosystem Integration contributes to SYNTHESIS
Volume of external system interactions from commit and build events
Health of active integrations from recent external events
Collective Intelligence contributes to SYNTHESIS
Insights derived from multi-project analysis via cross-pillar scores
Accuracy of collective intelligence from pillar score consistency
Logical Consistency contributes to COGNITION
Stable improvement counts across sessions
Steady accumulation of canon entries over time
Causal Reasoning contributes to COGNITION
Documented decisions in canon — reasoning captured
Improvement rate accelerates over sessions
Abstraction Ability contributes to COGNITION
Total patterns detected — ability to abstract from data
Variety of pattern types detected
Learning Rate contributes to COGNITION
Rate of new canon entries per session
Recent IQ growth faster than historical average
Decision Quality contributes to COGNITION
Number of documented decisions
Quality of commits — message length × tag presence
Assumption Tracking contributes to COGNITION
Canon entries serve as tracked assumptions
Number of predictions tracked — explicit assumptions
Inference Chains contributes to COGNITION
Patterns that reference other patterns — chained inference
Number of pillars with real data — broader inference base
Anomaly Detection contributes to COGNITION
Detect unusual commit churn spikes
Detect when a pillar diverges from the pack
Meta-Reasoning contributes to COGNITION
Multiple pillars scored — aware of its own structure
Canon entries about IGNIS itself — self-referential reasoning
Cognitive Load contributes to COGNITION
Event count is in a manageable range — not overwhelmed
Number of pillars with non-zero scores — cognitive breadth
Trend Extrapolation contributes to FORESIGHT
Low CV in session intervals — predictable cadence
Enough history to make reliable extrapolations
Risk Detection contributes to FORESIGHT
Number of pillars with negative trends — risk areas identified
Days since last commit — activity risk
Opportunity Mapping contributes to FORESIGHT
Active proposals — opportunities identified
Gap between weakest pillar and average — opportunity size
Scenario Quality contributes to FORESIGHT
Number of predictions — scenario generation volume
Variety of prediction types
Prediction Accuracy contributes to FORESIGHT
Enough predictions to evaluate accuracy meaningfully
Fraction of predictions that were verified correct
Calibration contributes to FORESIGHT
Session intervals are consistent — well-calibrated cadence
Prediction confidence levels match actual accuracy
Horizon Coverage contributes to FORESIGHT
Predictions cover different time horizons
Commit events cover sufficient time range
Early Warning contributes to FORESIGHT
Declining commit frequency detected early
Single pillar dropping while others rise — early warning
Predictive Value contributes to FORESIGHT
Predictions that lead to actions
Forge proposals align with predicted needs
Anticipatory Action contributes to FORESIGHT
Rate of forge proposals creation — anticipatory planning
Predictions followed by actions
Novelty Assessment contributes to CREATIVITY
Number of unique file paths touched — breadth of novelty
Variety of canon entry types — novel pattern recognition
Cross-Pollination contributes to CREATIVITY
Commits touch files in many directories — cross-module work
Predictions cover different domains
Brainstorm Fertility contributes to CREATIVITY
Total forge proposals — brainstorm pipeline volume
Canon entries growing — captured creative patterns
Implementation Creativity contributes to CREATIVITY
Unique files per commit — creative scope per change
Variety of commit types (feat, fix, refactor, etc)
Pattern Breaking contributes to CREATIVITY
Variation in commit frequency — non-routine patterns
Score volatility as proxy for creative risk-taking
Contrarian Analysis contributes to CREATIVITY
Growth in weakest pillar — contrarian to strengths-only approach
Refactor commits vs feature commits — contrarian maintenance
Serendipity Detection contributes to CREATIVITY
Score jumps that exceed trend prediction — serendipitous gains
Improvements in sessions that did not plan for them
What-If Generation contributes to CREATIVITY
Number of predictions — what-if scenarios generated
Forge proposals as what-if explorations
Innovation Pipeline contributes to CREATIVITY
Total items in innovation pipeline (proposals + predictions)
Innovation pipeline covers multiple domains
Creative Confidence contributes to CREATIVITY
Deep canon + patterns indicate creative depth
High forge acceptance rate — confident proposals
Insight Density contributes to EXPRESSION
Canon entries per session — insight capture rate
Commit messages contain meaningful detail
Recommendation Quality contributes to EXPRESSION
Total improvements recommended — recommendation output
Canon entries available for recommendations
Narrative Coherence contributes to EXPRESSION
Canon entries form a coherent knowledge base
Commit messages use consistent tagging — coherent commits
Communication Effectiveness contributes to EXPRESSION
Commit messages are informative (length + tags)
Sessions with audit notes — documented communication
Explanation Depth contributes to EXPRESSION
Memories available to enrich explanations
Canon entries with substantial descriptions
Voice Consistency contributes to EXPRESSION
Regular scoring intervals — consistent schedule
Consistent use of conventional commit tags
Warning Accuracy contributes to EXPRESSION
Velocity warnings correlate with actual outcomes
Weakest pillars actually improve — warnings lead to action
Evidence Quality contributes to EXPRESSION
Canon entries as accumulated evidence
Events with source URLs — traceable evidence
Audience Adaptation contributes to EXPRESSION
Different commit types for different audiences
All pillars scored — comprehensive for any audience
Meta-Communication contributes to EXPRESSION
Canon entries about scoring or meta-analysis
Predictions about IGNIS performance — meta-forecasting
Growth Trajectory contributes to POTENTIAL
Recent growth faster than overall — accelerating
Enough history to measure trajectory — data for growth
Capability Gaps contributes to POTENTIAL
Missing adapters — data source capability gap
Signal types not fully covered
Resource Utilization contributes to POTENTIAL
Predictions created and verified — prediction system utilization
Memories available — context system utilization
Ceiling Distance contributes to POTENTIAL
Distance from historical maximum — recovery potential
Theoretical score if all data were perfect — gap to ideal
Unlock Conditions contributes to POTENTIAL
Each new adapter unlocks more scoring — untapped adapters
More history unlocks better trend analysis
Scaling Readiness contributes to POTENTIAL
Canon system populated and growing — knowledge maturity
Regular session cadence established — scalable process
Latent Patterns contributes to POTENTIAL
Many signal types — potential for undiscovered correlations
Event volume vs canon size — patterns waiting to be found
Improvement Velocity contributes to POTENTIAL
Commit count per session — code delivery speed
Canon entries per session — knowledge capture speed
Architectural Capacity contributes to POTENTIAL
10-pillar architecture fully active — structural capacity
Cluster/factor architecture capacity — 1000 factors
Genome Readiness contributes to POTENTIAL
Canon entries as genome knowledge base
Canon pattern diversity — genome pattern depth
Event tag canon links to synthesis
Decomposition recommendation targets the current weakest pillar
Decomposition recommendation targets the current weakest pillar
Decomposition recommendation targets the current weakest pillar
Decomposition recommendation targets the current weakest pillar
Decomposition recommendation targets the current weakest pillar
